independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for testing tasks)

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `tests/test_role_propagation_e2e.py` exercises all three roles via the middleware-provided `start_async_task` tool (not direct graph invocation).
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC001RolePropagatesThroughMiddlewareTool is parametrised over ['critic','researcher','planner'] and invokes the actual middleware-provided start_async_task coroutine returned by deepagents.middleware.async_subagents._build_async_subagent_tools. The fake LangGraph SDK client substituted via patch.object(_ClientCache, 'get_async') routes the SDK roundtrip into the real compiled jarvis_reasoner.graph — no test directly calls graph.invoke. All 6 parametrised assertions pass.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC001RolePropagatesThroughMiddlewareTool is parametrised over ['critic','researcher','planner'] and invokes the actual middleware-provided start_async_task coroutine returned by deepagents.middleware.async_subagents._build_async_subagent_tools. The fake LangGraph SDK client substituted via patch.object(_ClientCache, 'get_async') routes the SDK roundtrip into the real compiled jarvis_reasoner.graph — no test directly calls graph.invoke. All 6 parametrised assertions pass.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Three parametrised cases: `role="critic"`, `role="researcher"`, `role="planner"`. For each: the test asserts the jarvis_reasoner graph's initial-state node received `role=<value>` AND that `ROLE_PROMPTS[RoleName(<value>)]` was resolved and injected as the system prompt.
criteria_verification.evidence: test_role_arrives_at_graph_initial_state asserts fake_client.captured_inputs[task_id]['role'] == role_value and ['prompt'] matches per role. test_role_prompts_resolves_and_injects_system_prompt asserts the wrapper graph's async_tasks[0]['role'] equals the input role AND that role_mocks[role_value].system_prompt == ROLE_PROMPTS[RoleName(role_value)] — proving compile-time injection of the right prompt — plus that the other roles' inner mocks were NOT awaited (no fan-out leakage).
criteria_verification.notes: test_role_arrives_at_graph_initial_state asserts fake_client.captured_inputs[task_id]['role'] == role_value and ['prompt'] matches per role. test_role_prompts_resolves_and_injects_system_prompt asserts the wrapper graph's async_tasks[0]['role'] equals the input role AND that role_mocks[role_value].system_prompt == ROLE_PROMPTS[RoleName(role_value)] — proving compile-time injection of the right prompt — plus that the other roles' inner mocks were NOT awaited (no fan-out leakage).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Correlation-id propagation: `input={"prompt": ..., "role": ..., "correlation_id": "abc123"}` — the test asserts `check_async_task(task_id)` returns a result carrying `correlation_id="abc123"` (scenario: *The session correlation identifier propagates from input through to check-task results*).
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC003CorrelationIdRoundTrip exercises both contracts: test_correlation_id_lands_on_async_tasks_channel asserts the wrapper graph's async_tasks payload carries correlation_id='abc123' verbatim; test_check_async_task_surfaces_correlation_id launches with correlation_id='abc123', invokes the real check_async_task coroutine through the SDK seam, parses the returned ToolMessage JSON to confirm thread_id + status, and re-asserts captured_results[task_id]['async_tasks'][0]['correlation_id'] == 'abc123'.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC003CorrelationIdRoundTrip exercises both contracts: test_correlation_id_lands_on_async_tasks_channel asserts the wrapper graph's async_tasks payload carries correlation_id='abc123' verbatim; test_check_async_task_surfaces_correlation_id launches with correlation_id='abc123', invokes the real check_async_task coroutine through the SDK seam, parses the returned ToolMessage JSON to confirm thread_id + status, and re-asserts captured_results[task_id]['async_tasks'][0]['correlation_id'] == 'abc123'.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Parallel safety: start two concurrent tasks with different roles (`critic` + `planner`); assert distinct task identifiers; assert each task resolved its own role prompt; assert neither overwrote the other's state (scenario: *Two different role-mode tasks can run in parallel without collision*).
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC004ParallelSafety::test_two_concurrent_tasks_resolve_independently uses asyncio.gather to launch a critic and a planner task simultaneously, then asserts critic_task_id != planner_task_id, each captured_results[*]['async_tasks'][0]['role'] matches its launch role, each correlation_id is preserved, and the unrelated 'researcher' inner mock was never awaited. test_concurrent_inputs_isolated_in_captured_state additionally checks captured_inputs preserves both prompts/cids/roles as distinct sets.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC004ParallelSafety::test_two_concurrent_tasks_resolve_independently uses asyncio.gather to launch a critic and a planner task simultaneously, then asserts critic_task_id != planner_task_id, each captured_results[*]['async_tasks'][0]['role'] matches its launch role, each correlation_id is preserved, and the unrelated 'researcher' inner mock was never awaited. test_concurrent_inputs_isolated_in_captured_state additionally checks captured_inputs preserves both prompts/cids/roles as distinct sets.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Uses `FakeListChatModel` with canned responses; zero real LLM calls.
criteria_verification.evidence: The role_aware_reasoner fixture instantiates FakeListChatModel(responses=...) and patches langchain.chat_models.init_chat_model to return it during the reasoner module's compile-at-import. TestAC005ZeroRealLLMCalls::test_fake_llm_cursor_unchanged_after_full_roundtrip asserts fake_llm.i == 0 after running both start_async_task and check_async_task end-to-end — the FakeListChatModel cursor advances on every invocation, so a 0 reading is proof of zero LLM calls. No provider client is instantiated either (init_chat_model is patched).
criteria_verification.notes: The role_aware_reasoner fixture instantiates FakeListChatModel(responses=...) and patches langchain.chat_models.init_chat_model to return it during the reasoner module's compile-at-import. TestAC005ZeroRealLLMCalls::test_fake_llm_cursor_unchanged_after_full_roundtrip asserts fake_llm.i == 0 after running both start_async_task and check_async_task end-to-end — the FakeListChatModel cursor advances on every invocation, so a 0 reading is proof of zero LLM calls. No provider client is instantiated either (init_chat_model is patched).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: If DeepAgents 0.5.3 preview API breaks role-key propagation at any point, this test fails loudly — it is the ASSUM-ASYNC-ROLE-PROPAGATION regression surface (if a 0.6 breakage occurs, the fallback is inlining `role` as a leading `[role=...]` token in the prompt per design §12; this test drives that decision).
criteria_verification.evidence: test_role_arrives_at_graph_initial_state contains a loud assertion message naming the assumption: 'role propagation broken: graph received {actual!r}, expected {expected!r}. ASSUM-ASYNC-ROLE-PROPAGATION breach — if this fires under deepagents>=0.6, fall back to inlining [role=...] inside prompt per design.md §12.' This message is the regression surface the 0.6 upgrade gate will read. The fake SDK client's runs.create routes input through the canonical extra-key shape the assumption requires the SDK to honour, so any future breakage of that contract surfaces here.
criteria_verification.notes: test_role_arrives_at_graph_initial_state contains a loud assertion message naming the assumption: 'role propagation broken: graph received {actual!r}, expected {expected!r}. ASSUM-ASYNC-ROLE-PROPAGATION breach — if this fires under deepagents>=0.6, fall back to inlining [role=...] inside prompt per design.md §12.' This message is the regression surface the 0.6 upgrade gate will read. The fake SDK client's runs.create routes input through the canonical extra-key shape the assumption requires the SDK to honour, so any future breakage of that contract surfaces here.

criteria_verification.evidence: Final run: `uv run pytest tests/test_role_propagation_e2e.py -v` → 12 passed in 0.90s. Full suite (`uv run pytest tests/`) reports 1387 passed, 2 skipped — zero regressions.
criteria_verification.notes: Final run: `uv run pytest tests/test_role_propagation_e2e.py -v` → 12 passed in 0.90s. Full suite (`uv run pytest tests/`) reports 1387 passed, 2 skipped — zero regressions.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `the stack-specific Phase-3 specialist` (Implementation)
BDD oracle: 2 scenario(s) reference step definitions that are not yet implemented. These do NOT block approval but should be implemented to make the scenarios runnable.
pending_examples: test_two_different_rolemode_tasks_can_run_in_parallel_without_collision — implement: All traceback entries are hidden. Pass `--full-trace` to see hidden and internal frames.
test_the_session_correlation_identifier_propagates_from_input_through_to_checktask_results — implement: All traceback entries are hidden. Pass `--full-trace` to see hidden and internal frames.
